videos.title: 9 Health Benefits Of Eating Raw Honey Every Morning | VisitJoy

transcript.fullText:
Hey everyone! Welcome to my channel. Today I 
want to talk about one of nature's sweetest foods - honey and go over exactly what 
happens inside your body when you eat it. Honey has a rich history and many 
health benefits that I'm excited to share with you. There's a lot 
of misinformation about honey, so I'll focus on credible scientific research 
and studies to bust some common myths. 1. Better Than Regular Sugar. When it comes to honey versus plain old sugar, honey actually has some advantages 
that might surprise you! Even though honey is sweet, it's not 
the same as sucrose or table sugar. The sugars in honey are absorbed a little 
more slowly, so your blood sugar doesn't spike as rapidly as chugging a soda. 
Honey also has antioxidants and healing compounds that white sugar totally lacks.
Research shows that eating a bit of honey daily can actually lower fasting blood 
glucose levels in folks with diabetes. How does it pull this off? Well, honey 
boosts a hormone called adiponectin that reduces inflammation and helps regulate blood 
sugar levels to keep them nice and steady. Now, this doesn't mean people with diabetes can go 
crazy eating honey! It still raises blood glucose, just not as dramatically. But used in 
moderation as a substitute for refined sugar, honey appears to be a wiser choice 
that may help control diabetes. Just make sure your honey is high quality and 
from a trusted source. Some dishonest companies fake honey by mixing in cheap corn syrup, which 
totally negates the health benefits! Go for raw, unfiltered honey from local beekeepers to 
get the real blood sugar-balancing deal. 2. Anti-Cancer Properties. When it comes to cancer-fighting foods, 
honey deserves some serious buzz! Early lab research indicates this sweet nectar 
may help combat cancer growth in several ways. Scientists have found that certain 
compounds in honey may block internal signals cancer cells use to multiply 
and spread. Honey basically confuses them and disrupts their communication, so 
they stop proliferating out of control! Studies also show honey can trigger an immune 
reaction, calling our body's natural cancer killers into action against malignant cells. In 
lab tests, honey suppressed the growth of lung, skin, colon, prostate, mouth, bone, 
and several other cancer cell types. However, we need more human clinical trials 
to prove honey's anti-cancer abilities. The preliminary laboratory results are exciting but 
don't start replacing chemo with honey just yet. For now, incorporating raw, local honey in 
a healthy diet provides general health perks but not definitive cancer-fighting 
effects. But future research on how honey interacts with tumors could 
lead to integrative treatments. Honey isn't a cancer cure-all yet, 
but the science is sweet so far! 3. Good For Heart Health. Early research shows that adding a little 
honey to your daily diet could help lower blood pressure and keep cholesterol levels 
in check. One big study found women who ate honey regularly were less likely to have 
high blood pressure. Research shows honey protects heart cells from oxidative 
damage and regulates heart rhythms. It seems those magical bees produce 
special compounds like antioxidants in honey that work like medicine for 
your heart. Raw honey contains propolis, a resin that studies indicate can lower 
unhealthy triglycerides and cholesterol. While more research is still needed, 
the initial findings suggest honey has some pretty sweet benefits for heart 
health! No single food is a miracle cure, of course. But incorporating 
local, unprocessed honey as part of an overall heart-healthy lifestyle 
could boost your cardiovascular system. 4. Helps in Asthma and Colds. People have used honey for ages to help 
soothe scratchy throats and nagging coughs. And now science is catching up and 
confirming this natural remedy really works! Research indicates honey may help 
calm asthma by reducing swelling in the airways. The antioxidants are thought to 
fight inflammation so you can breathe easier. Some studies also found honey can 
lessen coughing from chronic bronchitis. On top of that, honey has antimicrobial 
properties that may stop those nasty colds and flu bugs in their tracks 
before they totally knock you down. A spoonful of honey could help chase away 
fever, coughing, and congestion fast. Though more big studies are still needed, the 
current evidence suggests honey acts like a natural cough and cold syrup to suppress 
respiratory infections. It may not replace asthma inhalers or antibiotics, but honey seems 
promising for getting over the crud quicker. 5. Heals Burns and Wounds. People have used honey on wounds and burns for 
ages, and science is now proving it really works. Research on honey as a topical 
treatment found it most helpful for taking care of moderate burns 
you might get around the house or kitchen. It also helped heal stubborn 
wounds and infections after surgery. Manuka honey, in particular, worked 
wonders when applied right on burns. The sugars in honey may feed new skin cells, while the natural antibacterial compounds keep 
the wound clean and infection-free as it heals. Honey creates a moist environment too, 
which is ideal for growing fresh new skin. Now, honey should never replace emergency 
care for serious burns. Call 911 or seek help immediately for extensive burns. But 
for minor burns or stubborn surgical wounds, slathering on some honey could 
accelerate the healing process. More research is still needed, but 
the initial evidence is sweet - honey is far more than a delicious treat; it's a 
natural way to help your body heal faster! 6. Promotes Digestive Health. This natural sweetener does more than 
just satisfy your sweet tooth - it can also improve your digestion in key ways. First, honey contains enzymes that help break 
down carbs and other nutrients so your body can absorb them faster. Those natural sugars 
in honey are pre-digested, too, meaning they easily enter your bloodstream without taxing 
your digestive system like refined sugars do. Honey also contains prebiotics - 
non-digestible fibers that act as fertilizer for the good bacteria in your 
gut. Eating a bit of honey every day helps boost populations of those friendly microbes, 
helping maintain optimal digestive health. The prebiotics pass through your colon undigested, feeding your probiotics right where they 
live! A balanced microbiome equals better digestion and immunity. No wonder honey has been 
a digestive aid for centuries across cultures. So if you're feeling sluggish, try a spoonful of 
honey to get your digestive tract back on track. The enzymes boost energy while the prebiotics 
nourish gut bacteria for happy insides! 7. Improves Kid's Health. Want to help your kiddo grow up strong 
and smart? Try stirring a teaspoon of honey into their morning oatmeal! Research 
shows feeding children over one-year-old small daily doses of honey may boost 
brain power, growth, and well-being. Studies found honey-fed kids 
had better memory, less anxiety, and gained healthy weight compared 
to those given plain sterile water. Honey also reduced crying spells and was 
gentler on little tummies than regular sugar. Plus, it helped their bodies absorb more 
calcium for proper bone development. The skin benefits don't hurt either - honey left 
kids with clearer, more radiant complexions. Used in moderation, raw honey gives growing kids an 
array of nutrients absent from processed sweets. But don't go drizzling honey in your 
infant's bottle just yet! Honey should be avoided for the first 12 months due to the 
small risk of infant botulism. After one year, feel free to incorporate a teaspoon or 
two of honey daily into your toddler's diet. It's a healthier way to satisfy that 
sweet tooth and may boost their development. Just remember - no honey for 
babies under one! Beyond that, a touch of golden honey daily can 
set your kids up for sweet success. 8. May Help Acid Reflux. Some research suggests that honey may coat and 
soothe the irritated esophagus and stomach. This could stop acid and undigested food from 
creeping upwards and causing discomfort. However, no major studies have directly 
tested if honey reduces acid reflux. When stomach acid frequently flows back 
up into your esophagus, it causes what's known as gastroesophageal reflux disease or 
GERD. The symptoms are painful - heartburn, irritation, and regurgitation of stomach contents. The reasons behind acid reflux are complicated. 
While honey hasn't been proven to treat the root causes, adding a spoonful to your tea or yogurt 
is generally safe and may relieve symptoms. Those with chronic reflux issues should still 
see their doctor to discuss medications and lifestyle changes to manage them properly. But 
if you occasionally get hit with heartburn, add a spoon of honey to a glass of warm milk. 9. Antibiotic Resistance. Research shows honey can often tame 
coughs and soothe sore throats better than over-the-counter meds or 
prescription antibiotics. One review found it decreased cough frequency 
and severity more than antibiotics did. This is important because overusing 
antibiotics leads to resistance, meaning the drugs stop working on bacteria 
and viruses. But honey contains natural antimicrobial compounds to fight infections 
without creating resistant superbugs. Studies also revealed one special component 
in honey shows promise as a future antibiotic medicine! So honey has serious potential both as a 
natural remedy and for developing new meds later. Of course, honey shouldn't replace 
doctor-prescribed antibiotics if you have a serious bacterial infection that 
requires heavy-duty treatment. But for mild colds and coughs, a spoonful of honey 
offers a safe, inexpensive remedy to help you feel better faster and avoid 
unnecessary antibiotics when possible. In summary, introducing honey into your diet can 
positively impact many aspects of your health. While excessive sugar is unhealthy, 
honey's balanced fructose and glucose content offers unique therapeutic 
benefits with moderate consumption. Be sure to use raw, organic honey to get 
the most nutrients and reap the healing, protective effects documented 
in scientific research. Thank you for watching the video. If you 
like it, please share it with your friends and family. Please don't forget to subscribe 
to our channel and click the notification bell.

segments.text:
Hey everyone! Welcome to my channel. Today I 
want to talk about one of nature's sweetest

foods - honey and go over exactly what 
happens inside your body when you eat it.

Honey has a rich history and many 
health benefits that I'm excited

to share with you. There's a lot 
of misinformation about honey,

so I'll focus on credible scientific research 
and studies to bust some common myths.
1. Better Than Regular Sugar.
When it comes to honey versus plain old sugar,

honey actually has some advantages 
that might surprise you!

Even though honey is sweet, it's not 
the same as sucrose or table sugar.

The sugars in honey are absorbed a little 
more slowly, so your blood sugar doesn't

spike as rapidly as chugging a soda. 
Honey also has antioxidants and healing

compounds that white sugar totally lacks.
Research shows that eating a bit of honey

daily can actually lower fasting blood 
glucose levels in folks with diabetes.

How does it pull this off? Well, honey 
boosts a hormone called adiponectin that

reduces inflammation and helps regulate blood 
sugar levels to keep them nice and steady.

Now, this doesn't mean people with diabetes can go 
crazy eating honey! It still raises blood glucose,

just not as dramatically. But used in 
moderation as a substitute for refined sugar,

honey appears to be a wiser choice 
that may help control diabetes.

Just make sure your honey is high quality and 
from a trusted source. Some dishonest companies

fake honey by mixing in cheap corn syrup, which 
totally negates the health benefits! Go for raw,

unfiltered honey from local beekeepers to 
get the real blood sugar-balancing deal.
2. Anti-Cancer Properties.

When it comes to cancer-fighting foods, 
honey deserves some serious buzz!

Early lab research indicates this sweet nectar 
may help combat cancer growth in several ways.

Scientists have found that certain 
compounds in honey may block internal

signals cancer cells use to multiply 
and spread. Honey basically confuses

them and disrupts their communication, so 
they stop proliferating out of control!

Studies also show honey can trigger an immune 
reaction, calling our body's natural cancer

killers into action against malignant cells. In 
lab tests, honey suppressed the growth of lung,

skin, colon, prostate, mouth, bone, 
and several other cancer cell types.

However, we need more human clinical trials 
to prove honey's anti-cancer abilities. The

preliminary laboratory results are exciting but 
don't start replacing chemo with honey just yet.

For now, incorporating raw, local honey in 
a healthy diet provides general health perks

but not definitive cancer-fighting 
effects. But future research on how

honey interacts with tumors could 
lead to integrative treatments.

Honey isn't a cancer cure-all yet, 
but the science is sweet so far!
3. Good For Heart Health.

Early research shows that adding a little 
honey to your daily diet could help lower

blood pressure and keep cholesterol levels 
in check. One big study found women who ate

honey regularly were less likely to have 
high blood pressure. Research shows honey

protects heart cells from oxidative 
damage and regulates heart rhythms.

It seems those magical bees produce 
special compounds like antioxidants

in honey that work like medicine for 
your heart. Raw honey contains propolis,

a resin that studies indicate can lower 
unhealthy triglycerides and cholesterol.

While more research is still needed, 
the initial findings suggest honey

has some pretty sweet benefits for heart 
health! No single food is a miracle cure,

of course. But incorporating 
local, unprocessed honey as part

of an overall heart-healthy lifestyle 
could boost your cardiovascular system.
4. Helps in Asthma and Colds.

People have used honey for ages to help 
soothe scratchy throats and nagging

coughs. And now science is catching up and 
confirming this natural remedy really works!

Research indicates honey may help 
calm asthma by reducing swelling in

the airways. The antioxidants are thought to 
fight inflammation so you can breathe easier.

Some studies also found honey can 
lessen coughing from chronic bronchitis.

On top of that, honey has antimicrobial 
properties that may stop those nasty

colds and flu bugs in their tracks 
before they totally knock you down.

A spoonful of honey could help chase away 
fever, coughing, and congestion fast.

Though more big studies are still needed, the 
current evidence suggests honey acts like a

natural cough and cold syrup to suppress 
respiratory infections. It may not replace

asthma inhalers or antibiotics, but honey seems 
promising for getting over the crud quicker.
5. Heals Burns and Wounds.

People have used honey on wounds and burns for 
ages, and science is now proving it really works.

Research on honey as a topical 
treatment found it most helpful

for taking care of moderate burns 
you might get around the house or

kitchen. It also helped heal stubborn 
wounds and infections after surgery.

Manuka honey, in particular, worked 
wonders when applied right on burns.
The sugars in honey may feed new skin cells,

while the natural antibacterial compounds keep 
the wound clean and infection-free as it heals.

Honey creates a moist environment too, 
which is ideal for growing fresh new skin.

Now, honey should never replace emergency 
care for serious burns. Call 911 or seek

help immediately for extensive burns. But 
for minor burns or stubborn surgical wounds,

slathering on some honey could 
accelerate the healing process.

More research is still needed, but 
the initial evidence is sweet - honey

is far more than a delicious treat; it's a 
natural way to help your body heal faster!
6. Promotes Digestive Health.

This natural sweetener does more than 
just satisfy your sweet tooth - it
can also improve your digestion in key ways.

First, honey contains enzymes that help break 
down carbs and other nutrients so your body

can absorb them faster. Those natural sugars 
in honey are pre-digested, too, meaning they

easily enter your bloodstream without taxing 
your digestive system like refined sugars do.

Honey also contains prebiotics - 
non-digestible fibers that act as

fertilizer for the good bacteria in your 
gut. Eating a bit of honey every day helps

boost populations of those friendly microbes, 
helping maintain optimal digestive health.
The prebiotics pass through your colon undigested,

feeding your probiotics right where they 
live! A balanced microbiome equals better

digestion and immunity. No wonder honey has been 
a digestive aid for centuries across cultures.

So if you're feeling sluggish, try a spoonful of 
honey to get your digestive tract back on track.

The enzymes boost energy while the prebiotics 
nourish gut bacteria for happy insides!
7. Improves Kid's Health.

Want to help your kiddo grow up strong 
and smart? Try stirring a teaspoon of

honey into their morning oatmeal! Research 
shows feeding children over one-year-old

small daily doses of honey may boost 
brain power, growth, and well-being.

Studies found honey-fed kids 
had better memory, less anxiety,

and gained healthy weight compared 
to those given plain sterile water.

Honey also reduced crying spells and was 
gentler on little tummies than regular sugar.

Plus, it helped their bodies absorb more 
calcium for proper bone development.

The skin benefits don't hurt either - honey left 
kids with clearer, more radiant complexions. Used

in moderation, raw honey gives growing kids an 
array of nutrients absent from processed sweets.

But don't go drizzling honey in your 
infant's bottle just yet! Honey should

be avoided for the first 12 months due to the 
small risk of infant botulism. After one year,

feel free to incorporate a teaspoon or 
two of honey daily into your toddler's

diet. It's a healthier way to satisfy that 
sweet tooth and may boost their development.

Just remember - no honey for 
babies under one! Beyond that,

a touch of golden honey daily can 
set your kids up for sweet success.
8. May Help Acid Reflux.

Some research suggests that honey may coat and 
soothe the irritated esophagus and stomach. This

could stop acid and undigested food from 
creeping upwards and causing discomfort.

However, no major studies have directly 
tested if honey reduces acid reflux.

When stomach acid frequently flows back 
up into your esophagus, it causes what's

known as gastroesophageal reflux disease or 
GERD. The symptoms are painful - heartburn,
irritation, and regurgitation of stomach contents.

The reasons behind acid reflux are complicated. 
While honey hasn't been proven to treat the root

causes, adding a spoonful to your tea or yogurt 
is generally safe and may relieve symptoms.

Those with chronic reflux issues should still 
see their doctor to discuss medications and

lifestyle changes to manage them properly. But 
if you occasionally get hit with heartburn,
add a spoon of honey to a glass of warm milk.
9. Antibiotic Resistance.

Research shows honey can often tame 
coughs and soothe sore throats better

than over-the-counter meds or 
prescription antibiotics. One

review found it decreased cough frequency 
and severity more than antibiotics did.

This is important because overusing 
antibiotics leads to resistance,

meaning the drugs stop working on bacteria 
and viruses. But honey contains natural

antimicrobial compounds to fight infections 
without creating resistant superbugs.

Studies also revealed one special component 
in honey shows promise as a future antibiotic

medicine! So honey has serious potential both as a 
natural remedy and for developing new meds later.

Of course, honey shouldn't replace 
doctor-prescribed antibiotics if

you have a serious bacterial infection that 
requires heavy-duty treatment. But for mild

colds and coughs, a spoonful of honey 
offers a safe, inexpensive remedy to

help you feel better faster and avoid 
unnecessary antibiotics when possible.

In summary, introducing honey into your diet can 
positively impact many aspects of your health.

While excessive sugar is unhealthy, 
honey's balanced fructose and glucose

content offers unique therapeutic 
benefits with moderate consumption.

Be sure to use raw, organic honey to get 
the most nutrients and reap the healing,

protective effects documented 
in scientific research.

Thank you for watching the video. If you 
like it, please share it with your friends

and family. Please don't forget to subscribe 
to our channel and click the notification bell.

videos.title: Honey and Cinnamon: Benefits (Daily)

transcript.fullText:
The health benefits and taking honey and cinnamon
daily, and a recipe to use at home. 1. Honey and cinnamon have been used for thousands
of years as a natural medicine and health booster.
2. By taking a small dose of these ingredients every day, you provide your body with some
wonderful nutrients. 3. These nutrients include Vitamins A, B6,
C, E and K. Also with calcium, sodium, potassium, iron, magnesium, phosphorus zinc, niacin,
folate and riboflavin. 4. This often helps people to lose weight,
as they find their appetite begins to stabilise and they crave unhealthy foods less.
5. This mixture also provides a massive boost to the immune system. This fights off diseases
and infections, and makes you stronger. 6. It has also been linked to preventing cancer,
due to the powerful phytochemicals in raw honey.
7. In order to take this every day you will need the following ingredients:
250ml of Hot Spring Water ½ Teaspoon of Ceylon cinnamon
1 Teaspoon of honey. Method: Mix together well and drink this on
an empty stomach in the morning before breakfast. It is important than you use organic raw honey,
and Ceylon cinnamon as opposed to the more general cassia cinnamon. 8. The cinnamon will help to supress your
appetite, and prevent you from over eating. 9. If you do find yourself craving snacks,
and are trying to lose weight. Drink this mixture again between meals.
10. This will work wonders if you have a cold or flu infection, as the powerful antioxidants
will kill the bacteria or virus quickly. 11. You can also drink this before and after
exercising. They will give you an energy boost and cause your body to burn off more calories.
12. Honey and Cinnamon have been shown to treat insect bites, and itching skin. Simply
mix them together and apply the paste to the problem area.
13. This can also lower blood sugar levels and reduce insulin resistance. This makes
it excellent for diabetics. 14. Honey is much healthier for the body than
regular sugar. So I suggest replacing sugar in your diet with this for better health.
15. Drinking this mixture will also cause your hair to grow thicker, shinier and healthier
due to the nutrient content. 16. It has been shown to reduce the risk of
heart disease, and actively fights bad cholesterol. 17. Taking Ceylon cinnamon regularly has been
shown to help those suffering with Alzheimer’s disease, and improves mental function.
18. Some studies show that this can help to fight the HIV-1 virus.
19. Best of all, both of these ingredients are absolutely delicious and can be used on
any of your regular foods to add sweetness and flavour.
Thank you very much for listening, a like is always appreciated and remember to subscribe
for more healthy videos. I wish you great health, wealth and happiness

videos.title: The REAL Health Benefits of Garlic and Honey

transcript.fullText:
Brainy Dose Presents:
The REAL Health Benefits of Eating Raw Garlic and Honey for 7 Days! In this video, we are going to be talking
about the benefits of garlic and honey. Combining garlic and honey may seem a bit
strange. But, these two ingredients put together result
in an impressive immune-boosting substance that helps your body fight infection. The Benefits of Garlic Garlic is used around the world for adding flavor to savory dishes. But, it also provides a long list of health
benefits. Thanks to its powerful antibacterial and antioxidant
properties! Garlic is low in calories, but high in nutrients. It contains Manganese, Vitamin B6, Vitamin
C, Selenium and fiber. It reduces the risk of atherosclerosis, heart
disease, stroke, colds and the flu. It is also beneficial if you are suffering
from bronchitis, asthma, stomach ulcers, or parasites. As well as alleviating hay fever, traveler’s
diarrhea, bug bites and fungal infections. Garlic also contains organosulphur compounds
that have many health benefits. Including inhibiting cancer! Particularly prostate cancer and stomach cancer. The Benefits of Raw Honey The benefits of raw honey are spectacular! And it tastes absolutely delicious. Raw honey is packed with powerful antioxidants,
along with enzymes and minerals. Including Iron, Zinc, Potassium, Calcium,
Phosphorus, Magnesium and Selenium. It also contains Vitamin B6, Thiamin, Riboflavin,
and Niacin. These natural vitamins and minerals in raw
honey help lower cholesterol levels. Raw honey also helps to fuel your body and
mind, while making it easier to maintain a healthy weight. It also has a healthy glycemic index that
aids in better digestion. So, what happens when you combine raw garlic
and honey? The combined antibacterial and antioxidant
properties of garlic and honey make a potent home remedy for boosting your immune system. To make your garlic-infused honey natural
home remedy, separate 3 or 4 garlic heads into individual cloves. You can peel them, but you don’t have to. Put the cloves into a small jar, add 1 cup
of raw honey, and mix the ingredients until the garlic cloves are completely covered by
honey. Put the lid on the jar, and allow the mixture
to infuse for a few days. You can keep your garlic-infused honey natural
home remedy either in the fridge or at room temperature. For a daily immune system boost, take one
spoonful of raw honey and garlic in the morning on an empty stomach. Try it for 7 days! You’ll start to experience more energy,
and your body will be functioning at an optimal level of health. Make sure to let us know about the health
benefits of eating raw garlic and honey YOU experienced during your 7 day challenge! Share your story in the comments below! Thanks for watching! Please like and share this video; And subscribe
for more!

segments.text:
Brainy Dose Presents:
The REAL Health Benefits of Eating Raw Garlic
and Honey for 7 Days!

In this video, we are going to be talking
about the benefits of garlic and honey.

Combining garlic and honey may seem a bit
strange.

But, these two ingredients put together result
in an impressive immune-boosting substance
that helps your body fight infection.
The Benefits of Garlic
Garlic is used around the world for adding
flavor to savory dishes.

But, it also provides a long list of health
benefits.

Thanks to its powerful antibacterial and antioxidant
properties!
Garlic is low in calories, but high in nutrients.

It contains Manganese, Vitamin B6, Vitamin
C, Selenium and fiber.

It reduces the risk of atherosclerosis, heart
disease, stroke, colds and the flu.

It is also beneficial if you are suffering
from bronchitis, asthma, stomach ulcers, or
parasites.

As well as alleviating hay fever, traveler’s
diarrhea, bug bites and fungal infections.

Garlic also contains organosulphur compounds
that have many health benefits.
Including inhibiting cancer!
Particularly prostate cancer and stomach cancer.
The Benefits of Raw Honey
The benefits of raw honey are spectacular!
And it tastes absolutely delicious.

Raw honey is packed with powerful antioxidants,
along with enzymes and minerals.

Including Iron, Zinc, Potassium, Calcium,
Phosphorus, Magnesium and Selenium.

It also contains Vitamin B6, Thiamin, Riboflavin,
and Niacin.

These natural vitamins and minerals in raw
honey help lower cholesterol levels.

Raw honey also helps to fuel your body and
mind, while making it easier to maintain a
healthy weight.

It also has a healthy glycemic index that
aids in better digestion.

So, what happens when you combine raw garlic
and honey?

The combined antibacterial and antioxidant
properties of garlic and honey make a potent
home remedy for boosting your immune system.

To make your garlic-infused honey natural
home remedy, separate 3 or 4 garlic heads
into individual cloves.
You can peel them, but you don’t have to.

Put the cloves into a small jar, add 1 cup
of raw honey, and mix the ingredients until

the garlic cloves are completely covered by
honey.

Put the lid on the jar, and allow the mixture
to infuse for a few days.

You can keep your garlic-infused honey natural
home remedy either in the fridge or at room
temperature.

For a daily immune system boost, take one
spoonful of raw honey and garlic in the morning
on an empty stomach.
Try it for 7 days!

You’ll start to experience more energy,
and your body will be functioning at an optimal
level of health.

Make sure to let us know about the health
benefits of eating raw garlic and honey YOU
experienced during your 7 day challenge!
Share your story in the comments below!
Thanks for watching!

Please like and share this video; And subscribe
for more!

videos.title: Health Benefits Of Honey - How Honey Is Made - How Many Calories Is Honey

transcript.fullText:
Honey starts as the nectar of flowering plants, 
nectar consists mainly of water and sugars, and bees extract the nectar and store it in their 
stomach where it mixes with enzymes that transform it for storage. The bees then return to their 
hive and pass it to other bees where it then gets deposited into a honeycomb. Bees fan the honeycomb 
with their wings to speed up the evaporation process. Once most of the water has evaporated, 
the bees seal the honeycomb with a liquid from their abdomen which hardens into beeswax, allowing 
the honey to be stored, giving the bees a food source for the cold months of winter. Beekeepers 
that take care of bees then extract some of the honey and bottle it for human consumption. A 
tablespoon of honey contains about 64 calories, 17 grams of sugar, including fructose and glucose 
and it has many benefits. Higher quality honey that is raw and unfiltered contains antioxidants 
which help to protect cells from free radicals, these antioxidants include phenolic acid and 
flavonoids which can help fight pathogens, help reduce inflammation in the body and are 
associated with a decreased risk of a number of chronic diseases. Honey can be used as a natural 
sweetener as many people add it to tea, other hot beverages and even smoothies. It can also 
be used as a sugar substitute in some recipes, or it can be used in place of syrup on pancakes 
and waffles. Honey is sweeter than sugar so you may be able to use less of it, but it does have 
more calories than sugar so you should use it in moderation. Honey consumption has been linked to 
many other benefits such as less respiratory tract infections, supporting a healthy gut, as it has 
prebiotic compounds that are beneficial to the good bacteria in the gut. Honey does contain small 
amounts of many different vitamins and minerals that are beneficial to overall bodily functions, 
and many people use it as a cough suppressant. It's also been linked to a reduced risk of heart 
disease and may be beneficial to brain function, however more research needs to be done in these 
areas. One note, babies under the age of 1 should avoid honey as it has been shown to potentially 
cause a gastrointestinal condition. Good quality, raw and unfiltered honey can cost around $13 for 
a 32 ounce bottle, and china is the number one producer of honey. The bottom line with honey is 
that a raw unfiltered honey is best to consume. If you're someone that prefers natural foods 
it can be a good alternative to sugar and it does have positive antioxidant benefits. All 
right my friends, hopefully you got something out of this video, I do have more videos right 
there for you, till next time, I am out of here.

videos.title: Honey and Exercise | Benefits of Eating Honey For Workouts | #Honey #Exercise

transcript.fullText: what's up youtube i'd like to talk about honey today more specifically why i always recommend my clients to add honey into their diet regardless of either trying to lose body fat or trying to put on some mass we're going to cover why honey can be good for you for workouts and what the science says about honey and exercise let's get started honey especially raw is considered a superfood then why not add to your diet after kicking yourself in the gym and after a good workout honey can be taken any time of the day especially for either pre or post i've made a video on why you should add dextrose for post-workout shake with your protein a few weeks ago here's the link if you haven't watched it be sure to watch it because it's basically about the ingestion timing and benefits of supplementing simple sugar like dextrose when working out anyways honey can also be a great substitute for dextrose now i know a lot of people think honey is a no-no during diets but is honey really that bad in the past honey was considered to be a fattening food since it's high in sugar however honey's sugar content is mostly made up of fructose and glucose and in many recent studies honey was found full of flavonoids and antioxidants and can help you on inflammation level reduction and prevention against obesity related metabolic syndromes many studies indicate that honey is a healthy natural source of glucose and it has a lower glycemic index than white sugar and can assist in numerous ways before during and after a workout according to a published study from nutrients journal in 2019 honey contains over 500 enzymes as well as calcium iron magnesium phosphorus potassium vitamin c and various vitamin b's honey is also used in variety of medical research on liver protection antioxidant heart functions antibacterial immune system boost and much more number one honey supplementation may improve workout performance as we all know carbohydrates or glucose serves as a vital energy source when we exercise the fructose in honey may serve as an excellent replenishment source of liver glycogen reserves back to the studies that i just mentioned from nutrients journal on honey and sports performance stated that simultaneous ingestion of fructose and glucose which what honey is mainly made of may increase the oxidation capacity up to 75 percent it also said that honey can be a vital source for athletes who specialize in heavy endurance sports like cycling number two honey supplementation may also improve recovery from intense workouts if you're having difficult time recovering from training you may want to consider the possibility of glycogen exhaustion which can cause complications in process of muscle cell repairs a study in 2015 published in asian journal of sports medicine claimed that when athletes were prescribed honeyed water after training it showed improvement in glycogen metabolization which may aid in recovery by quickly and effectively replenishing glycogen and nutrients the study also studied that honey water can also be given to athletes as an excellent rehydration source number three studies have shown that honey also has a positive effect on bone density and increase in bone mass based on animal clinical trials in a 2014 study it showed that honey supplementation may protect against the stress effects induced by jumping exercises on rats bones number four athletes or those who participate in high intensity training can suffer from high levels of inflammation and changes in the intestinal gut environment it is also reported that people on high protein diet may also suffer from decrease in beneficial intestinal bacteria flavonoid which honey is full of has similar functions as prebiotics and can serve to aid videos.title: Top 10 Benefits of Drinking an Olive Oil, Lemon, & Honey Mixture

transcript.fullText:
olive oil lemon and honey looking at each 
one individually isn't anything special right it's not until you mix them all together that 
you begin to see all of the amazing benefits that this trifecta can have on the human 
body and that is exactly what we are going to talk about in this video be sure to watch 
until the end because there's something about this mixture that not too many people know 
about so if you're ready let's get into it just a quick reminder we are not doctors 
this video is for informational purposes only the trifecta of olive oil lemon and honey when 
combined in a beverage creates a mixture that can provide a super boost to multiple organs and 
systems of the human body olive oil is widely considered to be a superfood because it contains 
antioxidant antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties that can prevent obesity reduce heart 
problems and help with diabetes among many other amazing benefits natural lemon juice is known to 
contain vitamin c calcium and magnesium among many other powerful properties that work to strengthen 
the immune system and ward off infections although lemon juice tastes sour and acidic studies found 
that it actually has alkaline effects on the body once it's metabolized which provides 
your body systems with a detoxifying boost honey isn't only great for its naturally sweet 
and delicious taste it offers some amazing health benefits as well it's loaded with antioxidants 
that can lower blood pressure improve cholesterol lower triglycerides and reduce the risk 
of blood clot formation in your arteries not to mention it also has soothing effects that 
can relieve a cough or a sore throat as well so with that said i think you're already starting to 
see just how beneficial this combination of super ingredients can be when it comes to providing a 
boost to your body and overall health now let's dive even deeper into some of the therapeutic 
benefits that this dynamic mixture can provide first and foremost it can boost your energy levels 
with more energy comes more focus which in turns mean that you will increase your productivity and 
get more done throughout the day this mixture also has been shown to improve brain cognition now of 
course it may not make the average person smarter but it can work as a protector against 
cognitive decline in older adults several studies show a link between consumption 
of extra virgin olive oil and improved cognitive functioning and it may even reduce your 
risk of developing alzheimer's or dementia the ingredient that's most likely responsible for 
these cognitive benefits is olyropede which is a compound that has neural protective effects 
extra virgin olive oil has an especially high concentration of alurpine so that when mixed with 
lemon juice and honey it can protect your brain health and keep your mind sharp as you age it can 
also improve digestion this is important because it helps the stomach break down food in a way that 
your body is able to take in more of the nutrients rather than them being wasted away another major 
benefit is that it helps detox the liver one of the main responsibilities of the liver is to get 
rid of toxins that are produced within the body since the combination of olive oil lemon 
and honey helps detox the liver essentially this means that it helps detox the entire body 
while purifying red blood cells in the process another one of the primary benefits is that it 
helps fight infections these ingredients contain antioxidant antibacterial and antimicrobial 
properties which are crucially important when it comes to fighting infections and keeping them from 
entering the body not to mention it also helps to reduce inflammation inflammation is a root cause 
of many of the diseases that occur within the body including cancer the properties found in olive 
oil lemon and honey work together to limit inflammation and keep it under control another 
benefit is that it helps prevent kidney stones thanks to the components found in natural 
lemon juice one that we need to mention is citric which is a component that is found 
in citric acid it works to make urine less acidic and studies found that it can actually 
help break up small kidney stones as well you should also know that this mixture has 
anti-aging effects on your skin this is mostly due to the vitamin e that is found in olive oil when 
combined with vitamin c that is found in lemons a study found that these vitamins can reduce skin 
wrinkling and damage that occurs to sun exposure another important benefit is that it protects 
against urinary infections as we mentioned earlier this beverage helps improve digestion 
which in turn also helps prevent urinary tract infections as well this is primarily thanks to the 
antibacterial components found in lemon and honey and one of my favorite benefits is that it helps 
prevent obesity olive oil and honey are fairly high in calories so you may be surprised to hear 
that they are linked to lower rates of obesity for example one study found that those who use 
olive oil had lower obesity rates than those who used other oils while cooking olive oil helps your 
body reduce its lipotoxicity which is the amount of lipid cells in your body fat this can lead to 
an overall decrease in the amount of fat that's around your organs honey has been shown to improve 
cholesterol and decrease triglyceride levels this explains why this combination 
can be effective in preventing obesity just as long as it's consumed in moderation so 
to create this super beverage all you need is one cup of warm water one lemon one tablespoon of 
honey and 1 tablespoon of extra virgin olive oil cut the lemon in half and squeeze fresh 
lemon juice into your glass of water then add in 1 tablespoon each of olive oil 
and honey stir and mix it well until the ingredients are dissolved and unified then 
you can enjoy this powerful and delicious beverage that provides all of the health 
benefits that were mentioned in this video real quick guys do me a huge favor and hit the 
like button it really helps support the channel and i greatly greatly appreciate it and while 
you're down there go ahead and click the subscribe button as well because we have a ton of other 
videos on our channel that i think you will enjoy millions of people around the world are looking 
for natural ways to improve their overall health hopefully the information that you learned 
in this video can help you do just that with that said keep in mind that we are not doctors in 
this video should not be taken as medical advice again this video is for informational purposes 
only but hopefully you learned a thing or two and hopefully you're now one step closer to 
improving your overall health and thus improving the overall quality of your life if so be sure 
to let us know down in the comment section below and if you want to learn even more about this 
topic we do have a full guide on our website i'll drop a link to it right below this 
video down in the description thank you so much for watching all the way to the end have a 
blessed day and as always breathe easy my friend you

videos.title: When Can Babies Have Honey? Benefits, Risks and Tips

transcript.fullText: remember to subscribe and don't forget to hit the bell icon to never miss an update from first cry parenting honey for babies when is it safe to introduce honey is loved by all but this sweet nectar can be dangerous for your little one if given to them before one year of age know why why can't your baby under one year eat honey honey occasionally contains a spore of a bacterium known as the clostridium batulinum this causes a rare form of food poisoning in babies symptoms of which come to light around 8 to 36 hours after consumption heating boiling pressure cooking or pasteurizing do not eliminate these bacteria as they go unaffected and contaminate the honey babies aged 12 months and below can be victims since they have immunocompromised systems and are developing what is infant botulism infant botulism causes muscle weakness and breathing issues in babies when they ingest the bacteria through food sources like honey when can babies have honey pediatrician recommendations state that babies can consume honey only once they cross the 12 month mark and are over a year old consumption of pasteurized honey for babies below 12 months of age is also not recommended what are the benefits of honey for toddlers number one provides instant relief from cold flu and cough number two it is a storehouse of multivitamins essential amino acids minerals and flavonoids number three acts as a natural aid for sleep and eczema number four contains rich antibacterial and antifungal properties which lead to faster healing of wounds number five promotes cognitive development and improves mood levels what if honey is accidentally given to your baby if your baby is above 18 months of age it is not a problem since the baby's immune system is developed for the consumption of honey if your baby is below 12 months of age contact your doctor immediately as they may be susceptible to paralysis and death due to an immunocompromised system if your baby has already consumed honey look for signs of infant botulism such as listlessness lack of appetite lethargy and muscle cramps pressure cook homemade foods to eliminate any spores of the bacterium and reduce the chances of batolinium spores developing in their gastrointestinal tracts is it safe to use honey pacifiers no any amount of honey can be dangerous for a baby below 12 months of age it can cause a risk to your baby's health honey in different forms infants above the age of 18 months are typically safe from the effects of the botulism's paws and can be given honey in any form to remember these cautions and precautions and you are good to go

videos.title: 12 AMAZING BENEFITS OF HONEY FOR YOUR FACE

transcript.fullText: honey has been in use for centuries as a natural remedy for various skin conditions it is a natural humectant which means it attracts and retains moisture honey also contains antioxidants and enzymes that promote healing in this video you will learn the facial benefits of honey one honey has an antibacterial and antifungal effect on the face honey is a natural moisturizer that can cleanse and treat skin that bacteria and fungi have infected this syrup removes the dead cells on the skin surface and prevents the growth of bacteria which causes pimples and blackheads on the face the skin becomes soft and smooth after using honey you can easily remove the syrup with water 2. honey is a powerful antioxidant this syrup contains antioxidants that help fight the free radicals and prevent skin damage antioxidants also protect the skin from sun exposure by preventing the formation of wrinkles dark spots and dryness it is also used for treating acne and other skin disorders free radicals result from various chemical reactions in the body and are responsible for aging honey helps to neutralize free radicals and protects the skin from damage three honey helps to reduce the appearance of scars and stretch marks honey has anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce scarring and stretch marks on the skin it also helps restore elasticity in the skin and make it more youthful looking this property is because it contains essential vitamins and nutrients that help to keep the skin healthy and plump it also has anti-cancer properties that help to prevent the formation of new scars apply a few drops directly onto the skin or mix honey with your favorite moisturizer or lotion and apply it over the scars and stretch marks you can also mix honey with fresh rose water to soothe the skin and make it more youthful looking 4. honey can help the skin maintain its natural ph level which means it helps keep the skin's acid mantle the skin's ph level is vital because it affects how well it can retain moisture also it helps to prevent the skin from overreacting to certain external factors such as weather pollution and even specific products honey has a ph level of 7.35 which is slightly alkaline therefore it helps to maintain the skin's natural ph level skin with an acidic ph is more prone to damage in aging whereas skin with an alkaline or neutral ph level can better resist environmental aggressors 5. honey is a natural moisturizer the absorbent qualities of honey help keep the skin moist and hydrated and help repair damaged skin cells by nourishing them with essential nutrients necessary for cell regeneration the hygroscopic properties of honey help it draw moisture from the air and into the skin it can also lock in moisture by attracting water molecules bound to other surface molecules like collagen and elastin although this process is not as efficient as artificial moisturizers it can provide a large amount of lasting hydration 6. honey can help reduce itching and irritation of the skin caused by eczema or dermatitis the skin of people with eczema or dermatitis is very susceptible to infection and honey can help protect it from bacterial infection honey has antimicrobial properties that fight the growth of microorganisms and viruses on the skin the antimicrobial properties of honey are due to its high concentrations of hydrogen peroxide which is a powerful antibacterial compound honey also contains glucose fructose and sorbitol which help maintain the skin's natural ph balance and protect it from infection 7. honey can be used as a natural insect repellent although it is not as effective as a bug repellent like d-e-e-t which is the active ingredient in many commercial bug sprays honey can help ward off mosquitoes and other insects that may transmit diseases when applied to the skin honey acts as a barrier that prevents insects from laying eggs on your skin it is thought that the unique chemical composition of honey which includes fructose and sorbitol might be responsible for its effectiveness in repelling insects 8. it relieves pain and inflammation honey can aid in relieving pain and inflammation particularly for individuals who have suffered from burns or other injuries that have caused skin damage it is an all-natural treatment for pain and inflammation the syrup contains substances that can help reduce pain associated with inflammation and promote healing 9. honey helps in the formation of new cells and the repair of damaged ones by accelerating collagen production honey may play a role in the regeneration of cells and tissues by accelerating the process of collagen formation which is known to be important in the healing of wounds studies have shown that honey stimulates fibroblast growth which forms collagen the increase in fibroblast was seen at all stages of healing honey is also a natural antiseptic and can help clean wounds from bacterial infection 10. honey can reduce the number of pores on the face and make them less visible honey can make porous skin less visible by helping to reduce the number of pores on the face a study in japan has shown that honey can reduce oiliness on the skin and make pores less visible honey has an antibacterial effect and helps to reduce the bacterial buildup in pores 11. honey prevents the formation of wrinkles honey has a natural tightening smoothing and whitening effect on the skin it can help reduce wrinkles and fine lines to improve the skin's elasticity when the skin is exposed to sunlight it produces a natural substance known as melanin which causes dark spots the presence of this substance can cause the skin to become wrinkled using honey regularly can help keep the skin youthful and prevent wrinkles 12. honey helps treat acne the antibacterial properties of honey make it suitable for treating acne it contains potent antioxidants that help reduce the redness and irritation of the skin due to acne it also has an anti-inflammatory effect which prevents the formation of new pimples as you can see honey is a great natural product to use on the face it has many benefits such as cleansing and moisturizing the skin it also has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties which help to protect the skin from damage honey can be used alone or with other natural ingredients such as yogurt or milk so why not give honey a try your skin will thank you for it i hope you have enjoyed this video if you have please subscribe to this youtube channel and don't forget to give this video the thumbs up and i'm sure you're going to enjoy this next video on the health benefits of honey and turmeric thanks for watching and bye for now [Music]
